rulings.Case Title: CHARLES HUSBAND VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 20, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 6. Defendant York International Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Specifically, plaintiff¿s special interrogatory responses identified Steve Pelz as a person with knowledge in support of plaintiff¿s contentions and stated what knowledge he had and defendant¿s moving papers failed to demonstrate that he had no such knowledge. Plaintiff¿s responses also identified and summarized defendant¿s responses to General Order 129 interrogatories and the deposition testimony of defendant¿s corporate representative. The responses are not factually devoid and defendant¿s moving papers failed to address any of this information or demonstrate that the information cited and discussed does not support plaintiff¿s contentions against defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MEREDITH GORDON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 20, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 7. Defendant National Bulk Carriers, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting their causes of action against defendant. The limited number of special interrogatories that defendant submitted with its moving papers, which were not identical as to each plaintiff, are neither all facts interrogatories nor the equivalent of all facts interrogatories, neither were they asking for all witnesses and documents supporting those facts. Further, defendant only attached plaintiffs¿ responses to special interrogatory no. 1 and no more, so defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiffs¿ responses to any interrogatories other than no. 1 were factually devoid. (See Exhibits G and J.) Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY LIMA VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Granting Leave To Intervene Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 387
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 20, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Intervenors Hartford Fire Insurance Company, West American Insurance Company, Safeco Insurance Company of America, Travelers Indemnity Co., and Zurich American Insurance Co., Successor in Interest to Zurich Insurance Company, US Branch¿s motion for an order granting leave to intervene pursuant to CCP § 387 is granted. The Court has taken judicial notice of the letter from the Secretary of State and the website printout. Rich-Tex was a suspended company and the insurance companies are permitted to intervene. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES C. DAVIDSON et al VS. DOWMAN PRODUCTS, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Defendant Ford Motor Company, Inc.S Motion To Transfer Venue And Stay Proceedings Pending Transfer
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 20, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Defendant Kelly-Moore Paint Co., Inc.¿s joinder to defendant Ford Motor Company¿s motion to transfer venue and stay proceedings pending transfer: After considering oral arguments and the initial and supplemental papers, defendant¿s joinder is denied without prejudice. The underlying motion was taken off calendar and defendant¿s joinder failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. § 1005(b). Defendant may re-file to comply with the notice requirements of C.C.P. § 1005(b) and C.C.P. § 1010.6 if using electronic service. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)
